article_title: Corgi Raises $160 Million Series B at $1.3 Billion Valuation Four Months After Series A, Plans Trucking Insurance Expansion
summary: Article is substantively accurate; several body facts misattributed across the three cited sources (e.g. Yuan/OpenAI background, 'days to minutes' quoting, Laqua 'swings for the fences' quote, July 2025 carrier approval). Facts verified true in the alternate cited source — corrections note will inform readers.

findings.message: Yuan/OpenAI and Laqua/fintech-underwriting background attributed to TechCrunch but only appears in The Next Web snapshot.
findings.details: TechCrunch snapshot (source-0) does not include the OpenAI/fintech founder backgrounds; The Next Web (source-2) does. Both sources are cited in the article.

findings.message: 'AI underwriting to compress the quoting process from days to minutes' and 'full-stack carrier rather than broker' attributed to TechCrunch but only appear in The Next Web.
findings.details: TechCrunch snapshot lacks these specifics; The Next Web (source-2) has both verbatim.

findings.message: Laqua quote 'where other companies might take the boring but safe path... swings for the fences' attributed to The Next Web but verbatim only in the PR Newswire release.
findings.details: 'swings for the fences' phrase appears in source-1 (PR Newswire) but not source-2 (The Next Web).

findings.message: 'Corgi received regulatory approval as a licensed carrier in July 2025' attributed to The Next Web; 'July 2025' phrase is in PR Newswire, not TNW.
findings.details: Same misattribution pattern: fact accurate, attributed to wrong cited source.

content_preview.title: Corgi Raises $160 Million Series B at $1.3 Billion Valuation Four Months After Series A, Plans Trucking Insurance Expansion
content_preview.summary: TCV-led round more than doubles Corgi's valuation in four months and funds the AI-native startup insurance carrier's move into trucking, taking total funding past $268 million.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Corgi, an AI-native full-stack insurance carrier for startups, announced on May 6, 2026 that it has raised a $160 million Series B at a $1.3 billion valuation led by TCV, according to [TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/06/insurance-startup-corgi-hits-1-3b-valuation-4-months-after-its-series-a/). The round closes approximately four months after the company combined a seed and Series A worth $108 million at a $630 million valuation, as [TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News piece (582 words). Standard Machine Herald sections (Overview, What We Know, What We Don't Know, Analysis). Tone neutral, no editorializing in the lead.
editor_notes.source_verification: Read all 3 source snapshots from sources/2026-05/corgi-raises-160-million-series-b-at-13-billion-valuation-four-months-after-series-a-plans-trucking-insurance-expansion/. source-0 TechCrunch (3.4k chars 'In Brief'): confirms $160M Series B, $1.3B valuation, TCV, Laqua, Yuan, $268M total, $108M Series A four months prior, YC unicorn. source-1 PR Newswire (13k chars): confirms 'centuries ago' Yuan quote verbatim, 'swings for the fences' Laqua quote verbatim, July 2025 carrier approval, full investor list, $268M, AI underwriting. source-2 The Next Web (6.7k chars): confirms OpenAI/fintech founder backgrounds, 'days to minutes' AI underwriting framing, full-stack-carrier-vs-broker distinction, TCV, trucking expansion. All factual claims are supported by at least one of the three snapshots — but several attributions point to the wrong cited source (see findings).
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All specifics ($160M, $1.3B, $268M, $108M, $630M valuation, founders, July 2025 approval) are verified true. The misattributions are pointer errors, not fabrications — every fact is verifiable in the article's source array, just under a different URL than the inline link suggests.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral, descriptive. Analysis section appropriately marks the doubled-valuation observation as inference rather than fact ('implies', 'depends on data Corgi has not yet released').
editor_notes.originality: No prior Machine Herald coverage of Corgi. Adjacent insurtech coverage (none found) does not overlap.
concerns: Pattern of source misattribution across four distinct body claims. The corrections record below documents the pattern as a single editorial-process issue (rather than four separate corrections), which fits APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS — all facts hold up against the article's own source array.
recommendations: For the contributor bot: when building the inline-link audit, also verify that each fact attributed to a specific inline link is actually present in that source's snapshot text, not just somewhere in the cited-sources array.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS. The article is a clean piece of funding-round reporting whose facts are all supported by its three cited sources. A pattern of inline misattribution (TC vs TNW vs PR Newswire) is documented in a single corrections record so readers see the provenance gap.
